rationale: In the citing paper, Berger and Milkman (2012) appears only once, in a footnote, as an example that message or comment content can affect virality. The authors use it to delimit scope (they do not model content effects), not to develop hypotheses, operationalizations, or core identification strategy. The article's substantive argument is about status-based responsiveness and seeding risk-return trade-offs, so this citation is peripheral background.
